title: RNA isolation for tissue
steps: Homogenization in TriZol Add 1ml 1 mL, Trizol per 30 mg, tissue and homogenize using handheld homogenizer.
Incubate at RT for 00:05:00 to allow nucleoprotein complexes to dissociate.
Spin down at max speed in a chilled centrifuge for 00:15:00 .
Carefully remove the top aqueous phase and transfer to a new Eppendorf tube. The interphase and bottom organic phase can be saved for DNA and protein respectively.
To the aqueous phase, add 500 µL of 100% isopropanol, mix by inversion and incubate at -20 °C for a minimum for 02:00:00 .
Spin down at max speed for 00:30:00 to precipitate RNA.
Remove supernatant, and add 1 mL, 75% Ethanol to wash the pellet.
Add 200 µL, Chloroform carefully, and vortex to mix well.
Spin down at max speed for 00:15:00 and remove supernatant.
Resuspend pellet in appropriate volume of nuclease free water.
creator: Chin Yee Tan
